api: CoinGecko REST API v3 (https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3, pro: https://pro-api.coingecko.com/api/v3)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, scoped deliberately to the single-coin (coin_id-configured) resource family this connector's name/design targets (matching legacy internal/connectors/coingecko-coins, which only ever implemented coin/market_chart/history) -- not the full CoinGecko API (search, exchanges, derivatives, NFTs, on-chain data, categories, global stats, simple/price, coins/markets, coins/list), which is a distinct, much larger product surface with no per-connection coin_id/base_url scoping shape in common with this bundle and is out of scope for this connector. Within the coin_id-scoped family: coin and tickers are implemented; market_chart and history remain blocked (ENGINE_GAP, unchanged from the prior unblock pass); ohlc was newly reviewed this pass and confirmed to hit the identical array-of-tuples ENGINE_GAP as market_chart (see docs.md Known limits) -- not a new distinct gap, but independently confirmed rather than assumed.

excluded.reason: ENGINE_GAP: zips 3 parallel [timestamp,value] arrays (prices/market_caps/total_volumes) into one record per timestamp; the dialect has no array-zip/parallel-array-join primitive. See docs.md Known limits.
ENGINE_GAP: same parallel-array-zip shape as market_chart, plus a client-supplied from/to Unix-timestamp range with no bearing on the underlying record-shape gap; see docs.md Known limits.

ENGINE_GAP: response is a bare array of [timestamp, open, high, low, close] tuples (a JSON array, not an object) per array element; connsdk.RecordsAt's []any case only keeps elements that decode as map[string]any, so a bare-tuple array element is silently dropped, yielding zero records -- the identical root-cause limitation as market_chart's array-zip gap (no array-of-arrays/tuple-to-record primitive), confirmed in this pass rather than assumed. See docs.md Known limits.
ENGINE_GAP: same bare-tuple-array shape as ohlc; see docs.md Known limits.

ENGINE_GAP: legacy issues one independent GET request per calendar day between start_date and end_date (the date query param IS the loop variable, not a next-page/cursor token); the dialect's pagination types all advance from response state, incremental issues exactly one request per read with a server-side lower-bound filter, and fan_out requires a discoverable/configured id list -- none can express 'N independent single-day requests over a client-computed date range'. See docs.md Known limits.
token-contract-scoped coin lookup, a distinct addressing scheme (asset-platform + contract address, not coin_id) from this connector's coin_id-scoped design
contract-address-scoped variant of the blocked market_chart array-zip shape; out of scope for the same ENGINE_GAP reason plus the contract-address addressing scheme mismatch above
contract-address-scoped variant of the blocked market_chart array-zip shape; out of scope for the same ENGINE_GAP reason plus the contract-address addressing scheme mismatch above
